issues.title: Schema doc falsely says ai_router currently uses budget.yaml for spend reporting
issues.description: The new canonical schema doc's example comment said ai_router uses ai_router/budget.yaml for spend reporting, but no Python runtime reader exists (the doc itself says so in its Readers section) — internally contradictory and factually wrong. The comment had been carried over verbatim from the retired adoption-bootstrap.md example.
issues.resolution_note: Example comment rewritten: the file is read procedurally by the session orchestrator (workflow doc Step 6) to pick the verification path; no ai_router code parses it at runtime today; automated threshold monitoring / pre-call warnings are planned future work. Now consistent with the doc's own Readers section and the S1 §2.3 audit.

issues.title: Cross-repo notice has a broken internal step reference
issues.description: The closing paragraph said 'the habit in step 4' but the recommended habit is step 5 — step 4 became the 'What a clean upgrade looks like' section when it was inserted mid-session (after the consumer's upgrade report) without renumbering the closing reference.
issues.resolution_note: Reference corrected to 'the habit in step 5'.

issues.title: Evidence bundle is incomplete/internally inconsistent for the claimed new docs
issues.description: The R1 evidence's whole-tree `git diff --stat` omitted docs/budget-yaml-schema.md, docs/cross-repo-router-version-lag-notice.md, and the per-set UAT checklist because all three were still untracked (git diff does not show untracked files), so the round-1 prompt could not substantiate that the deliverables were in the change set.
issues.resolution_note: All three files git-added (now `A` in `git status --short`); they existed on disk throughout — this was an evidence-bundle gap in the verification prompt, not a missing deliverable. R2 evidence includes the staged-status listing showing the additions.
